title: Why is AI expensive all of a sudden?
summary: Alberta Tech examines why AI inference has become expensive, focusing on the trend of "tokenmaxxing" where models are deliberately padded with extra tokens to increase billing. The video references industry analysis from The Pragmatic Engineer blog and explores the rise and fall of this practice and underlying cost drivers.
writeup: Alberta Tech examines why AI inference costs have risen, centering on "tokenmaxxing," the practice of deliberately padding model outputs with extra tokens to inflate billing. The video draws on industry analysis from The Pragmatic Engineer blog to trace the rise and fall of this practice and the underlying cost drivers. Key concepts include token spend as a billing lever and local inference as an alternative.
